title: sida semidentata
intro: Sida semidentata, also known as the hairy sida or hairy mallow, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) and is native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sida semidentata include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use in wound healing and as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a decoction or made into a tea, or it can be used in the form of a paste or powder in traditional medicine.
